problems.goal: write a hello world program in C. Use clang to compile it and run it.
write a Python todo list manager that stores todos in a SQLite database. Support add, list, and delete operations. Include a test file using pytest.
render a line on screen via SDL2. Use sdl2-config in the Makefile to set up SDL2 libs.
write the fibonacci sequence using C#. Use the dotnet command to compile C#.
write a Go HTTP server with a GET /ping endpoint that returns JSON {"status":"ok"}. Use the Gin framework. Include a Makefile.
write a Rust command-line program that prints the first 10 Fibonacci numbers. Use cargo to build and run.

write a Python REST API using Flask with a SQLite backend. Support POST /todos (body: JSON with a "task" field) and GET /todos (returns list of todos). Include a pytest test file that tests both endpoints. Provide a Makefile that installs dependencies with pip and runs pytest.

write a Node.js todo list manager that stores todos in a JSON file. Support add, list, and delete operations via a CLI. Include a test file using Jest. Provide a Makefile that installs dependencies with npm and runs the tests.

write a Vue 3 TypeScript todo list web app. Use Vite as the build tool and Vitest for unit tests. The app should display a list of todos and allow adding new ones via a text input and button. Include a test file using @vue/test-utils that tests adding and displaying todos. Provide a Makefile that installs dependencies with npm and runs the tests.

write a blog application with an ASP.NET Core minimal API backend and a Vue 3 TypeScript frontend. Backend: use EF Core with SQLite, define a Post model with Id, Title, and Content, seed one example post titled 'Hello World' on startup, expose GET /api/posts returning JSON. Include an xUnit test project using WebApplicationFactory that calls GET /api/posts and asserts the 'Hello World' post is present. Frontend: Vue 3 TypeScript app built with Vite that fetches and displays posts, with a Vitest test that mocks fetch and asserts the post is rendered. Provide a Makefile with an install target (npm install in frontend/) and a test target that runs dotnet test and npx vitest run.

build a Foucault pendulum demo in C using SDL2. Open an SDL2 window and animate a Foucault pendulum: a bob swinging from a pivot whose plane of oscillation slowly precesses over time, redrawing the scene each frame. Run for a fixed number of frames (e.g. 600) and then exit cleanly returning 0, so the program terminates on its own without user input. Use sdl2-config in the Makefile to set the SDL2 compiler and linker flags.

build a 2D physics demo in C using SDL2 illustrating gravity and collision detection. Spawn several falling items of different shapes - balls and boxes - that rotate as they fall. Each frame, apply gravity, advance positions and rotation, and detect collisions between items and with the ground and walls. On each collision, compute a new velocity direction and spin for the items involved, reducing their speed and spin a little each time (damping) so energy decreases over time. Animate every frame in an SDL2 window. The simulation ends when all items have come to rest lying still on the ground, at which point the program exits cleanly returning 0 (damping guarantees it settles, so it terminates without user input). Use sdl2-config in the Makefile to set the SDL2 compiler and linker flags.
